# 3 keys to successful biopharma-academia drug development collaborations

By Dave GreenwaldMarch 31, 2023

![A business person and a scientists walk around a stack of books with beakers and numbers in the background. -- First Opinion coverage for STAT](https://www.statnews.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/AdobeStock_334811079-645x645.jpeg)

Academia can be an excellent source of novel drug targets and new technologies that can enhance medicinal discovery. Indeed, academic inventors or founders contributed to more than [one-quarter of all medicines](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC8552459/) approved from 2001 through 2019.

Yet many sponsored research collaborations that hope to tap into [academia](https://www.statnews.com/2022/11/10/tipping-point-is-coming-unprecedented-exodus-of-young-life-scientists-shaking-up-academia/) fail. Academic-industry partnerships present a unique set of challenges that require thoughtful setup and dedicated execution from both the company and academic institution.

Advertisement

While there are many important details of a successful biopharma-academia collaboration, I see three key aspects that biopharma companies should take into account in order to increase the probability of a successful endeavor:

## Covering Cutting-Edge Scientific and Business Trends in Drug Delivery

Pharma, biotech and drug delivery industries will gather at the **15th annual** PODD event to assess delivery needs, explore partnership opportunities, and stay at the forefront of innovative drug delivery technologies. This includes small molecules, biologics, combination products, connected devices, cell and gene delivery and more.  
  
PODD provides **partnering opportunities** through organized networking for new, emerging and established collaborations.

![](https://d38uvx7mib76ry.cloudfront.net/public/images/87831d1e3e26904059dd4ccfe07a72c9de8de103/54120243842_f3f5294c9e_c.jpg)

## 2025 Drug Delivery Technology Track Options

![](https://d38uvx7mib76ry.cloudfront.net/public/images/b24305875839f28346f1b5704594777a26d279e3/54121386028_d015934ba4_c.jpg)
![](https://d38uvx7mib76ry.cloudfront.net/public/images/c1b39e7d2819aceb7dee34f45ad7191c74b7e973/54121096671_45267c7c47_c.jpg)

## Additional Program Features

•  **Convenes 1,000+ Attendees**from Pharma, Biotech, Drug Delivery, Academia, Investors and More  
•  **Pharma Company Spotlights** on Drug Delivery Needs and Partnering Philosophies  
•  **Technology Presentations – 10 Tracks** Covering a Wide Range of Drug Delivery Technologies  
•​​​​​​​  ​​​​​​​**Partnering Opportunities** that include One-on-One Scheduled Meetings through the PODD App to Help Foster New Collaborations

## The PODD Promise

**1.** Present a strategic-level program for pharma and biotech scientists and business development professionals, with a thorough overview on formulation needs, the latest drug delivery technologies available along with an update on deals and opportunities to improve therapies and extend the life cycle ​​​​​​of a drug.  
​​​​​​​  
**2.** Provide drug delivery and specialty pharmas with a platform to  
​​​​​​​present their technologies.  
  
**3.** Offer ample networking time and services for one-on-one meetings to establish new business contacts and enhance existing ones.

![image placeholder](https://d38uvx7mib76ry.cloudfront.net/public/images/0fdaad2756d463c1493b7583f518ab2372145a82/54121429089_3834cb3b32_c.jpg)

enriched_points.expanded_content: Collaborations between academic institutions and pharmaceutical companies enhance drug development by leveraging innovative research and financial resources, significantly improving success rates in bringing new drugs to market.

Integrating diverse fields such as engineering, biology, and computer science fosters innovative approaches in drug delivery systems, leading to groundbreaking technologies and improved patient outcomes.
International collaborations focus on addressing global health challenges, enhancing drug accessibility, and ensuring equitable distribution of new therapies through shared knowledge and resources.

Increased funding from governmental and private sectors supports innovative projects, driving advancements in drug delivery technologies that enhance efficiency and patient care across healthcare systems.
Innovative drug delivery systems improve treatment efficacy and patient adherence, ultimately reducing healthcare costs and enhancing overall health outcomes in diverse populations.
